message:
chore(beads): close ft-rsqap partial + file ft-mw1zb

5/12 deny paths wired via gate helper in c3d273f0. Per-site wiring for
the 7 direct authorize call sites filed as ft-mw1zb, blocked on
ft-eu0no landing so the edits on WaGetText/WaSearch/WaSend/WaWorkflowRun
don't collide with pane 2's in-flight SharedRateLimiter refactor.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -237,6 +237,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-meg4s","title":"simd_scan: OutputScanState string_phase field missing from 3 constructors blocks frankenterm-core build","description":"Commit 99703277 added a string_phase field to simd_scan::OutputScanState but did not update three constructor sites that still use struct-literal syntax without the field. Result: frankenterm-core does not compile under --no-default-features (E0063 at simd_scan.rs:476, :825, :842). Fix: add string_phase: StringPhase::default() (or equivalent) to all three constructors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:15:23.868401Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:42.746013Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:42.745488Z","close_reason":"Landed in 79029eeb (feat(simd_scan): wire ansi_state_step into all scan variants + 8 regression tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-mejto","title":"MCP tool/resource manifest golden — protocol conformance freeze","description":"Freeze the MCP server's public protocol surface via a golden manifest test: tool names + description/input-schema hashes, resource URIs + description hashes, resource-template URIs + description hashes. Any tool addition, removal, description change, or input-schema change must explicitly update the golden. Prevents silent protocol drift for downstream MCP clients.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:23:19.218909Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:33:25.408221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T05:33:25.408008Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","golden","mcp","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-mw1nw","title":"escape-parser hyperlink: Display does not escape : = ; in params/uri, breaks Display→parse roundtrip","description":"frankenterm/escape-parser/src/hyperlink.rs:101-116 Display impl writes param keys, values, and the uri raw, without escaping the OSC 8 separators (:, =, ;). Hyperlink::parse at line 87-92 splits on : then =, so any param key or value (or uri) containing those characters is mis-parsed: a param value like 'user:id' is split into a fake second param 'id' with no = and fails with 'bad params', failing the ENTIRE hyperlink parse and silently dropping the link. Two TODOs at lines 104 and 110 flag this but no fix has landed. Minimal fix: either reject invalid characters at new/new_with_params/new_with_id construction time (debug_assert or Result-returning constructor), or percent-escape the bad characters in Display and percent-decode in parse. A regression proptest (Display → parse of arbitrary Hyperlink) would surface this on the first fuzz case where a URI contains ':' or a param contains ';'.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:11:14.661154Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:14:35.710473Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:14:35.710128Z","close_reason":"Regression fence landed in 92378703 (test(hyperlink): pin OSC-8 embedded-separator parse failure). Concurrent-agent commit landed the exact same test I wrote. The underlying fix (Display escaping or try_new_with_params validator) remains an open design choice — reopen a new bead when a maintainer picks the direction. The regression test now asserts current broken behavior so any drift trips loudly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-mw1zb","title":"[security][follow-up ft-rsqap] Wire 7 direct authorize deny paths to policy_denied_audit (blocked on ft-eu0no)","description":"## Context

ft-rsqap partial ship in c3d273f0 wired 5 of the 12 deny paths via mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation (the shared gate helper used by wa.tx_run/rollback + wa.mission_pause/resume/abort).

Remaining 7 direct `.authorize(&input)` sites in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs where the Deny arm still only returns MCP_ERR_POLICY without writing to policy_denied_audit:

- :1216 wa.get_text (Mux, read)
- :1626 wa.search (Mux, read)
- :2184 wa.send (Mux, write — deny path; PolicyGatedInjector already audits the success path to audit_actions, different table)
- :2435 wa.workflow_run (Workflow, write)
- :3385 wa.reserve (Swarm)
- :3536 wa.release (Swarm)
- :3795 wa.accounts_refresh (Swarm)

## Scope

Each of the 7 handler call() methods already lives inside a `runtime.block_on(async { ... })` with a StorageHandle in scope, so the async `storage.record_policy_denial_audit(record).await` path works directly — no blocking bridge needed like the one added in storage.rs:record_policy_denial_audit_blocking for the sync gate.

Pattern per site:

    let decision = engine.authorize(&input);
    if decision.is_denied() {
        let reason = policy_reason(&decision).unwrap_or("...").to_string();
        let record = PolicyDeniedAuditRecord {
            id: 0,
            ts_ms: i64::try_from(now_ms()).unwrap_or(0),
            agent_id: None,
            tool_name: "wa.get_text".to_string(),
            intent_hash: Some(intent_hash_hex(&summary)),
            reason: reason.clone(),
            reason_code: PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::REASON_CODE_DENIED.to_string(),
            rule_id: decision.rule_id().map(String::from),
            decision: PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::DECISION_DENIED.to_string(),
        };
        let _ = storage.record_policy_denial_audit(record).await;
        tracing::warn!(target: "ft::security::policy", ...);
        return Err(...);
    }

## Blocker

Pane 2's ft-eu0no is still uncommitted (+70 lines on mcp_tools.rs touching exactly the 4 non-gate tools: WaGetText/WaSearch/WaSend/WaWorkflowRun). Wiring those 4 sites today collides with their SharedRateLimiter threading work. The other 3 (wa.reserve/release/accounts_refresh) may be safe to wire now but splitting the 7 across two beads would be bead-churn.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every one of the 7 sites writes a PolicyDeniedAuditRecord on Deny AND RequireApproval before returning the MCP_ERR_POLICY envelope.
- A per-surface integration test (one for Mux/Workflow/Swarm) that seeds a deny-by-default policy and asserts the row shape.
- docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md status column flipped to ✓ on all 8 rows.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-rsqap partial, 2026-04-23.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:18.370069Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:18.370069Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","follow-up","mcp","policy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-n2j5w","title":"Fix MCP module pre-existing test errors under default features and verify 170 test functions pass","description":"# Goal
Fix the known test compilation/runtime errors in mcp.rs that prevent the 170 test functions from running with default features enabled.

# Background
MEMORY.md documents: mcp.rs has pre-existing test errors with default features. The MCP module (3,229 lines, 170 #[test] functions) has comprehensive test coverage. MCP submodules (mcp_bridge, mcp_framework, mcp_helpers, etc.) are declared inside mcp.rs via #[path], NOT in lib.rs — this means feature-gate interactions may affect module resolution.

# Design
## Diagnostic steps
1. Run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp 2>&1 | tee /tmp/mcp_errors.log
   (Using build recipe: CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<agent>-target)
2. Categorize errors:
   - Type mismatches from recent module changes -> update test expectations
   - Missing types/imports due to feature gates -> add #[cfg(feature)] or fix import paths
   - Async runtime errors (#[tokio::test] vs asupersync) -> migrate to sync or runtime_compat
   - Stale test data referencing old API shapes -> update fixtures
3. Fix each error, verifying after each fix that the change compiles
4. Final run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp -- all 170 tests green

## Common failure patterns to watch for
- MCP submodules use #[path] declarations — if a submodule file moves or renames, the path attribute breaks
- The 57 tokio-based test failures (ft-22x4r) may overlap — if MCP tests use #[tokio::test], coordinate with ft-682n6
- Feature flag interactions: some test helper types may only exist under specific features (mcp, subprocess-bridge, etc.)
- robot_types.rs changes may have updated MCP response envelope shapes without updating mcp.rs test expectations

## Structured Logging
- Diagnostic log: mcp_test_fix_<timestamp>.log with each error, category, fix applied
- Before/after test counts to verify no tests lost

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp passes with 0 failures under default features.
2. All 170 test functions execute (none skipped, none #[ignore]d without documented reason).
3. No semantic changes to test intent — only update expectations to match current API.
4. Diagnostic log documenting each fix for future reference.
5. If any tests require non-default features, they must have explicit #[cfg(feature = "...")] annotations with comments explaining why.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:08.516219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.705038Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.704865Z","close_reason":"Verified: 201 MCP tests pass with zero failures under default features (cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp::tests). The pre-existing test errors documented in memory were likely fixed by other agents. No code changes needed — the 170 test functions mentioned in the bead have grown to 201, all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fix","mcp","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-n38nt","title":"mux proptest_tab_wire_serde test target fails to compile (PaneNode: Clone + 15-tuple prop_map)","description":"The `cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --test proptest_tab_wire_serde` target currently fails to compile at HEAD with two independent errors:

1. `error[E0277]: the trait bound PaneNode: Clone is not satisfied` at proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:108-111 — `arb_pane_node`'s `Just(PaneNode::Empty)` and `arb_pane_entry().prop_map(PaneNode::Leaf)` require `PaneNode: Clone` for proptest's `Strategy` impl. `PaneNode` only derives `Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq, Debug` at frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:3906.

2. `error[E0599]: the method `prop_map` exists for tuple (..., × 15), but its trait bounds were not satisfied` at proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:68 — `arb_pane_entry` builds a 15-element tuple but proptest 1.11's `Strategy` impl only extends to some smaller arity.

Both errors exist independently of ft-lapw8's split-test restoration. The test file has therefore been silently broken since at least commit 095ca88a. Surfacing this so the proptest suite is actually run in CI rather than silently failing to build.

Fix direction:
- Add `Clone` to `PaneNode` derive. `PaneEntry`, `SplitDirectionAndSize`, `SplitDirection`, `SplitSize`, and `Box<PaneNode>` already support Clone — the transitive closure is clean.
- Refactor `arb_pane_entry`'s 15-element tuple into a nested tuple (`((a,b,c,d,e,f,g), (h,i,j,k,l,m,n,o))`) or a fixture struct + `prop_map_each` to stay within proptest's tuple-arity limit.

Evidence: cargo test output at audit time shows 3 errors (8 error spans) under --no-default-features defaults with `CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane6-target`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:56.034014Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:30:39.493294Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:30:39.493003Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","compile","mux","proptest","test-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-n3mim","title":"[mor:ergonomics] Error::Runtime(String) is the '3am diagnostic dead-end' variant","description":"# Observation

Memory and grep confirm the frankenterm-core Error enum has no
\`Generic\` variant — the convention is \`Runtime(String)\` for
ad-hoc failures. Every time a caller needed a fresh error shape and
didn't want to define a typed variant, they reached for
\`Error::Runtime\`.

At 3am during an incident, the operator sees:

    Error: runtime: mux pool write failed: io error: Broken pipe (os error 32)

This is a String wrapped in an enum variant. You can't match on it,
you can't branch on it programmatically, you can't build a
dashboard row off it. The information that would make the incident
tractable — is this a transient network blip, a crashed mux, a
kernel panic, a rate limit, a disk-full? — is buried in a
string the operator has to grep for.

# Question

For each subsystem that currently stuffs into \`Error::Runtime(...)\`,
is there a typed variant that would make triage faster? Specific
candidates worth auditing:

- \`runtime_compat\` cancel / timeout wrappers — currently stringify
  the underlying failure into Runtime
- \`mux_pool\` write/read failures that aren't already covered by
  \`WeztermError\`
- \`asupersync\` lifecycle errors the caller needs to distinguish
  from transient I/O

A principle: every error the operator would act on differently
deserves a typed variant. Everything else can stay generic.

# 3am impact

This is the class of issue that doesn't show up in normal workflow.
It only bites when production is on fire. The cost is invisible
until the cost is enormous. The fix is incremental: every Runtime
creation site is a candidate for typing, and typing them one-at-a-
time pays immediately in the next incident that touches that
subsystem.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:36:27.030250Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.450481Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:57.270015Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["3am","ergonomics","error-types","mor"],"comments":[{"id":3600,"issue_id":"ft-n3mim","author":"jemanuel","text":"Commit 79702a50 introduces typed top-level runtime/pane/watchdog error variants plus source enums in crates/frankenterm-core/src/error.rs, deprecates Error::Runtime as a last-resort catch-all, and migrates clean operator-facing call-sites in crates/frankenterm-core/src/export.rs, crates/frankenterm-core/src/search_explain.rs, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs away from stringly Runtime(...). Added regression coverage for typed pane/watchdog/runtime classification and mock WezTerm error context. Local verification: rustfmt + git diff --check passed on the touched files. Requested rch lane (structured_error_ test prefix) is still in full-workspace sync on worker vmi1227854 with no compile/test result line yet. Note: crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs still has unrelated live edits in this checkout, so I intentionally did not widen this bead into that file.","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:57Z"}]}
@@ -294,7 +295,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-rpez9","title":"[reality-gap] README tx examples target a top-level CLI that the shipped ft binary does not expose","description":"README.md documents top-level `ft tx ...` commands in the command matrix and troubleshooting examples, but the shipped binary at `FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft` rejects `ft tx --help` with `error: unrecognized subcommand 'tx'`.

Observed reality:
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help` has no top-level `tx` command.
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft robot --help` advertises `tx plan|run|rollback|show` under robot mode.
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft mission plan --contract mission.json` also fails because the live flag is `--mission-file`, not `--contract`.

Impact: an operator following README examples gets an immediate parse failure from the shipped binary.

Expected fix:
- Update README tx examples to the live robot surface (`ft robot tx ...`).
- Use the live flag spelling (`--contract-file`) where a contract path example is shown.
- If a top-level human tx surface is intended, ship it and re-verify the binary before documenting it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:21.541365Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:50:06.280823Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:50:06.280129Z","close_reason":"AS-VERIFIED, no README edit needed. The bead's two concrete gaps were already closed by commit 3b9838ce (docs(readme): correct tx and config examples, same-day):

(1) bare 'ft tx ...' → 'ft robot tx ...' at README:158 (Quick Example tx run), :161 (tx show), :225 (Safety Guarantees bullet), :1230 (FAQ section). Current grep for bare 'ft tx ' matches zero lines.

(2) '--contract' → '--contract-file' at the matching lines. Current grep for '--contract' (not '--contract-file') matches zero lines. Cross-checked against the clap wiring: RobotTxCommands::Plan/Run at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:3173-3184 bind '#[arg(long)] contract_file', matching the README.

Note on bead context: the bead cited './FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help' showing no top-level 'tx' subcommand, but the live source at main.rs:430-443 actually defines a top-level 'Tx { command: TxCommands }' variant — that app bundle was stale. Either way the current README uses 'ft robot tx' consistently, which is also valid. No ambiguity left.

Did not touch README.md; respected GreenWren's exclusive reservation (expires 2026-04-23T20:42Z).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-rpjud","title":"[review][LOW] ft-eze48 InflatedDimension wire_mode capped at MAX_DIMENSION=32, misses u16::MAX memory-pressure case","description":"commit 6567397b added a fuzz WireMode::InflatedDimension variant at fuzz/fuzz_targets/ftvi_index_from_bytes.rs:191-196:

    WireMode::InflatedDimension(seed) => {
        if bytes.len() >= 8 {
            let dimension = u16::from((*seed).max(1)) % ((MAX_DIMENSION as u16) + 1);
            bytes[6..8].copy_from_slice(&dimension.to_le_bytes());
        }
    }

seed is u8. dimension = u16::from(seed.max(1)) % 33 → range [1, 32]. This is EQUAL TO OR LESS THAN the MAX_DIMENSION cap already used for generated records, so 'Inflated' is a misnomer — the written dimension is never actually inflated beyond what records could carry.

The intent of InflatedDimension is to test the parser's defense against a header claiming e.g. dimension=65535 with count=1 record — which would naively cause the parser to allocate 65535 * 2 bytes per record = 128 KB per record, scaling to memory-pressure attacks if count is also large.

Current code can never produce that attack shape because dimension is clamped to 32.

Fix (one-line):

    let dimension = u16::from((*seed).max(1)).saturating_mul(1024).max(MAX_DIMENSION as u16 + 1);

OR simpler: use the full u16 range:

    let dimension = u16::from_le_bytes([seed.wrapping_add(33), 0xFF]);

Either lets the parser's allocation-size handling be exercised against hostile headers. If the parser does allocate naively, libfuzzer with AddressSanitizer or memory ceilings would flag OOM crashes.

Compare to the parallel InflatedCount variant at :182-189, which uses saturating_add(extra+1) and thus CAN inflate count toward u32::MAX. Inflated dimension should follow the same pattern.

Severity: LOW — the raw-bytes Arbitrary lane (Input::Raw at :62-65) can generate arbitrary u16 dimension values directly, so this adversarial shape is reachable without the Structured lane. But the Structured lane is meant to BIAS toward hitting the interesting corruption shapes with valid surrounding headers, and an under-strength InflatedDimension dilutes that bias.

Audit round 14, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, fuzz, ftvi, wire-corruption.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:38:06.110440Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:52:07.591291Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:52:07.590412Z","close_reason":"Already shipped in 87afb28e: widen InflatedDimension to u16 hostile header values","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-rrrn5","title":"[SEV] CommandBuilder env_map_serde silently collides duplicate keys after to_string_lossy","description":"frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs env_map_serde (added in 803356bf) maps BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry> -> JSON via OsString::to_string_lossy for keys. Two distinct OsString keys whose non-UTF-8 bytes differ but collapse to the same String under lossy conversion will (1) produce a JSON map containing duplicate keys during serialize (most JSON parsers keep last, silently dropping one entry), and (2) on deserialize the BTreeMap::insert loop overwrites on collision, again silently dropping entries without returning an error. The adapter offers no detection or rejection of this case — a corrupted env set roundtrips to a smaller env set with no diagnostic. Metamorphic property: |deserialize(serialize(m))| == |m| — currently violated for any m with keys that collide under to_string_lossy. Conformance concern: the commit message asserts 'POSIX env names are ASCII-only' but setenv(3) on Linux accepts non-ASCII bytes (LC_*=ja_JP.UTF-8 etc.), and libc-level execve passes raw bytes. Fix by either (a) erroring on lossy conversion in both directions, or (b) serializing as an array of [key_bytes, value] pairs via serde_bytes / base64 so the roundtrip is lossless. At minimum add a proptest that asserts roundtrip preserves map size when two synthesized OsString keys share a lossy-equivalent representation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:30.464855Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:35:11.565957Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:35:11.565787Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 7a452b96: replaced map adapter with sequence-of-pairs adapter so every distinct OsString key survives serde roundtrip losslessly (via OsString's native byte/UTF-16-preserving enum encoding in value position). Added 2 regression proptests: one generates arbitrary byte keys (many non-UTF-8) and asserts all distinct keys roundtrip with paired values intact; the other injects two different invalid-UTF-8 sequences that both lossy-render to U+FFFD and asserts both survive. Test-binary run is blocked by pane6-target lock contention; fix is structurally complete and compiles clean at my previous build pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-rsqap","title":"[security][follow-up ft-hg6io] Wire 12 authorize deny paths to record_policy_denial_audit (blocked on ft-eu0no)","description":"## Context

ft-h90rh landed storage infra (4595cc0e): policy_denied_audit table + StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit.
ft-hg6io landed the wiring audit matrix (98b8f3d4): docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md enumerating every authorize call site, their deny paths, and the current audit-write gap.

What remains: actually call record_policy_denial_audit on every Deny / RequireApproval. 12 distinct deny paths identified in the matrix.

## Blocker

policy.rs and mcp_tools.rs are both heavily contested by pane 2's ft-eu0no SharedRateLimiter refactor (+81/-16 on policy.rs, +70 on mcp_tools.rs uncommitted as of 2026-04-23). This bead should wait for ft-eu0no to land so the wiring doesn't collide.

## Scope

Follow the sequenced plan in docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md:

1. Add policy.rs `build_policy_denial_record(decision, tool_name, agent_id, intent_hash) -> Option<PolicyDeniedAuditRecord>` helper with a unit test.
2. Refactor crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs::mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation to return a GateOutcome enum carrying the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord; each of the 5 callers writes the row in their deny branch inside the existing runtime.block_on async context.
3. Wire the 7 direct authorize sites (rows 2-8 in the matrix) to build and write a PolicyDeniedAuditRecord on deny.
4. Add a per-tool round-trip integration test: seed deny-by-default policy, call the tool, SELECT FROM policy_denied_audit, assert row shape.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every one of the 12 deny paths emits both tracing::warn! (already in place via ft-6mmyp) AND a row in policy_denied_audit.
- Round-trip test passes for at least one tool per surface (Mcp / Mux / Workflow / Swarm).
- docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md status column flipped from partial/missing to ✓ on every row.

## Split option

If the above is too big for one bead, split per the matrix into:
- ft-\<A\>: policy.rs helper + unit test
- ft-\<B\>: mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation GateOutcome + 5-caller wiring
- ft-\<C\>: 7 direct-site wiring + per-tool tests

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while scoping ft-hg6io, 2026-04-23.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:51.196904Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:52:43.064467Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","follow-up","mcp","policy","security"]}
+{"id":"ft-rsqap","title":"[security][follow-up ft-hg6io] Wire 12 authorize deny paths to record_policy_denial_audit (blocked on ft-eu0no)","description":"## Context

ft-h90rh landed storage infra (4595cc0e): policy_denied_audit table + StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit.
ft-hg6io landed the wiring audit matrix (98b8f3d4): docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md enumerating every authorize call site, their deny paths, and the current audit-write gap.

What remains: actually call record_policy_denial_audit on every Deny / RequireApproval. 12 distinct deny paths identified in the matrix.

## Blocker

policy.rs and mcp_tools.rs are both heavily contested by pane 2's ft-eu0no SharedRateLimiter refactor (+81/-16 on policy.rs, +70 on mcp_tools.rs uncommitted as of 2026-04-23). This bead should wait for ft-eu0no to land so the wiring doesn't collide.

## Scope

Follow the sequenced plan in docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md:

1. Add policy.rs `build_policy_denial_record(decision, tool_name, agent_id, intent_hash) -> Option<PolicyDeniedAuditRecord>` helper with a unit test.
2. Refactor crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs::mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation to return a GateOutcome enum carrying the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord; each of the 5 callers writes the row in their deny branch inside the existing runtime.block_on async context.
3. Wire the 7 direct authorize sites (rows 2-8 in the matrix) to build and write a PolicyDeniedAuditRecord on deny.
4. Add a per-tool round-trip integration test: seed deny-by-default policy, call the tool, SELECT FROM policy_denied_audit, assert row shape.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every one of the 12 deny paths emits both tracing::warn! (already in place via ft-6mmyp) AND a row in policy_denied_audit.
- Round-trip test passes for at least one tool per surface (Mcp / Mux / Workflow / Swarm).
- docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md status column flipped from partial/missing to ✓ on every row.

## Split option

If the above is too big for one bead, split per the matrix into:
- ft-\<A\>: policy.rs helper + unit test
- ft-\<B\>: mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation GateOutcome + 5-caller wiring
- ft-\<C\>: 7 direct-site wiring + per-tool tests

 {"id":"ft-ry224","title":"headless_mux_server: JoinFederation has no peer-count cap (unbounded DoS) + clobbers first_seen_at on rejoin","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:402-416 RemoteRequest::JoinFederation blindly inserts into self.peers without checking a cap, and overwrites the existing PeerInfo entirely on every re-join — losing first_seen_at. Two distinct defects:

1. **Unbounded peer-map growth (DoS)**: ServerConfig has max_connections (for clients) and max_panes, but NO max_peers. A hostile or buggy counterparty spamming JoinFederation with distinct node_ids grows self.peers without bound, eventually OOM'ing the mux server. prune_unreachable_peers only fires after peer_timeout_ms + an external caller — a burst of N spurious JoinFederation requests can bloat the map before any prune runs. Parallel shape to ft-kfy99 (tmux_cc line cap), ft-phz7x (codec oversize tagged_len), ft-8ihuz (scrollback unbounded read).

2. **first_seen_at lost on rejoin**: Every JoinFederation fully replaces PeerInfo at line 404-414, including setting first_seen_at to epoch_ms(). A peer that legitimately rejoins (e.g. partition heal after the Heartbeat ft-lekgj path tells it to re-JoinFederation) loses its original first_seen_at — making 'how long has this peer been federated' observability lie.

Fix: add max_peers: u32 to ServerConfig (default 512, twice max_connections as headroom). In JoinFederation, if the node_id is NEW and self.peers.len() >= max_peers, return RemoteResponse::Error { code: "peer_registry_full" }. If the node_id already exists, preserve first_seen_at from the existing entry and only update the re-join-time fields (node, status, last_heartbeat_at, capabilities). Regression tests: (a) 512-peer cap rejects the 513th distinct JoinFederation with peer_registry_full; (b) a rejoin keeps first_seen_at of the original insert.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:39:20.982908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:41:25.477529Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:41:25.476570Z","close_reason":"Landed in 78721403 (fix(headless_mux_server): cap JoinFederation peer registry + preserve first_seen_at on rejoin). Added ServerConfig.max_peers (default 512) + JoinFederation rejects new peers past cap with peer_registry_full; re-joins of known node_ids still succeed (they don't consume a slot). Also preserves existing first_seen_at on rejoin. Two regression tests pin both contracts. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
